prompt: A sculptor's hands pressing a highly detailed three-dimensional clay landscape model firmly downward onto a flat stone surface, photographed from the side. The clay model above shows rich topographic detail — mountain ridges, valleys, clustered settlements in distinct groups. The flattened impression being made on the surface below preserves the broad regional groupings and outlines clearly but loses the vertical relief and fine texture. Both the original 3D form and the emerging 2D impression are visible in the same frame. Natural studio lighting, dust and clay particles in the air. Photorealistic, shot on medium format film, soft directional light from one side casting gentle shadows. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no annotations, no symbols.
metaphor: Sculptor pressing a 3D clay landscape into a flat impression: groupings survive the compression but vertical detail is lost, mirroring structure-preserving dimensionality reduction.
